channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Guam and Northern Mariana Islands Prepare for Typhoon Bavi
item.description: Typhoon Bavi, a tropical cyclone barreling through the Pacific Ocean, was forecast to pass near the U.S. territories on Monday with the force of a Category 5 hurricane.
item.dc:creator: Josie Moyer and Mar-Vic Cagurangan

media:content.media:description: A man walking past boarded-up stores in Guam on Sunday.

item.title: Searching for What Pretty Means After an Acid Attack
item.description: Nafiah Ikram was steps from her home when a stranger threw acid in her face. Five years later, she is trying to rebuild her life and cope with a stunning twist in her case.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Latson

media:content.media:description: Nafiah Ikram, 27, was walking toward her home in March 2021 when a person threw acid in her face. She has undergone many surgeries since.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 5, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: ‘Near the End of the Meal, He Mentioned That He Was a Musician’
item.description: More friends of Metropolitan Diary, including the chef Daniel Humm and the writer Jennifer Egan, help mark the column’s 50th anniversary by sharing their New York City stories.

item.title: En su ‘Lux Tour’, Rosalía es un camaleón de la danza
item.description: Nadie confundiría a Rosalía con una bailarina de ballet profesional. Sin embargo, en el espectáculo de la ‘Lux Tour’, se acerca al ballet con lo que parece un interés y un respeto genuinos.
item.dc:creator: Margaret Fuhrer

item.title: It Failed in France. It Would Be a Disaster in California.
item.description: California might bet its future on the notion that billionaires won’t leave. But they are already fleeing the state.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Rauh and Benjamin Jaros

item.title: Israel Is Not Invincible
item.description: There is suddenly daylight between the Trump administration and Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Mairav Zonszein
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: In Fourth of July Speech, Trump Celebrates America and Derides Foes
item.description: A centerpiece address for the nation’s 250th anniversary celebration devoted time to American history, but had all the hallmarks of a Trump rally.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh

media:content.media:description: President Trump shared the stage with flags that he described with historical stories during his speech on the National Mall.

item.title: Inside ‘Chinese Dreamcore,’ Where Gen Z Relives a Brighter Past
item.description: Faced with a dire job market, falling wages and endless competition, many young Chinese are recreating scenes from the early 2000s on the internet, when the economy was growing rapidly.
item.dc:creator: Lily Kuo and Pei-Lin Wu

item.title: Rebel Catholics Defy Vatican’s Calls to Return to Mainstream Church
item.description: The Vatican has excommunicated the priesthood of a rebel Catholic faction. Many of the priests’ followers have refused to renounce them.
item.dc:creator: Elisabetta Povoledo

media:content.media:description: A procession on Wednesday by the traditionalist Society of St. Pius X in Écône, Switzerland.

item.title: Ordering Objects at V&E East Storehouse: From David Bowie Guitar to Beatrix Potter Originals
item.description: Visitors at the V&A East Storehouse can book appointments with artifacts that usually aren’t on show — and can even touch many of them.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall and Max Miechowski

item.title: Impeachment Trial of Philippine Vice President Set to Begin
item.description: Sara Duterte, who has said she would run for president, could be barred from politics over allegations of corruption and for making death threats against President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.
item.dc:creator: Jason Gutierrez

media:content.media:description: Vice President Sara Duterte of the Philippines in Manila, in February. Ms. Duterte has been impeached twice, and her trial is set to begin on Monday.

item.title: As Xi Looks to Extend His Rule, He Leans on a Longtime Ally
item.description: Xi Jinping is preparing to extend his rule as China’s leader and bring fresh blood into the Communist Party elite. Cai Qi is the man he’s tasking to help.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley
item.category: Politburo Standing Committee of the Communist Party of China

media:content.media:description: Cai Qi, a trusted deputy of the Chinese leader, Xi Jinping. He is probably the most influential official in Mr. Xi’s inner circle.

item.title: Chinese Dreamcore Takes Over Gen Z Social Media Feeds
item.description: Chinese Dreamcore is taking over the social media feeds of young people in China, who face falling wages and a dire job market. The memes and videos express a desire to return to simpler times.
item.dc:creator: Jiwoong Hong

item.title: Quote of the Day: Discord in Iran as Hard-Liners Resist U.S. Talks
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, July 5, 2026.

item.title: The Brooklyn Bridge Briefly Catches Fire During a Fireworks Display
item.description: The fire appeared to have been extinguished by shortly after 10 p.m., the police said. No injuries were reported.
item.dc:creator: Chelsia Rose Marcius, Mack Liederman and Nate Schweber

media:content.media:description: Associated Press

item.title: This California Town Banned Fireworks. Then Came the Dogs.
item.description: People flock to the coastal town of Cambria, which has become known for its fireworks ban, to give their dogs some respite on the Fourth of July.
item.dc:creator: Mimi Dwyer

media:content.media:description: Some dog owners hoping to escape the sound of fireworks are spending Independence Day in Cambria, Calif.

item.title: Nancy Pelosi’s Husband Could Face Charge Over Napa County Car Crash
item.description: The police said Paul Pelosi was the driver of a convertible that struck a parked car on Friday. The district attorney will decide whether Mr. Pelosi will be charged.
item.dc:creator: Maia Spoto

media:content.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Paul Pelosi, the 86-year-old husband of former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi, could be charged after striking a parked car on Friday. He told investigators that he knew he had hit something but didn’t know what.

item.title: Crowds Evacuated as Storms Menace Washington Ahead of Trump Speech
item.description: Thousands of people stood in security lines for hours in severe heat to reach the National Mall, only to be ordered to leave as events were set to start. Some refused.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson and Aishvarya Kavi

media:content.media:description: Thunderstorms headed toward Washington on Saturday evening, forcing evacuations of thousands at the National Mall.

item.title: Maryland Boat Explosion Leaves at Least 10 Injured, Officials Say
item.description: A 32-foot-long boat exploded in Chesapeake City, leaving four people with serious burns, officials said. The cause was under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Mack Liederman

item.title: United States Celebrates 250th Birthday
item.description: Americans celebrated the country’s 250th anniversary on Saturday with a series of Fourth of July events.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:credit: Rachel Parsons for The New York Times

item.title: The City Where You’ll Nearly Always Want a Jacket on the Fourth of July
item.description: As the East Coast bakes under a heat wave, San Francisco will be 66 degrees. But good luck watching the fireworks through the fog.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

media:content.media:credit: Christie Hemm Klok for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Cole Valley neighborhood of San Francisco last month.

item.title: Justice Department Defends Dropping Charges Against Indian Billionaire
item.description: An agency official said that Gautam Adani’s offer to invest $10 billion in the United States did not affect the decision to abandon a bribery case against him.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:credit: M. Scott Brauer for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gautam Adani, an industrial tycoon in India, was indicted with seven other co-defendants in 2024, in the last weeks of the Biden administration.

item.title: Boxed Sets
item.description: Rich Katz’s latest Sunday puzzle woos us with some lovely triangles.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Matthew Staver for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Solvers, don’t get lost in the weeds!

item.title: Canada Has a New Obsession: Soccer
item.description: A surprising World Cup run ended on Saturday against Morocco, but the Canadian successes, as a team and a host, are likely to endure.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja

media:content.media:credit: Ian Willms for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Fans cheered on players as Canada played Bosnia and Herzegovina in Toronto last month in the first game of the 2026 World Cup set in Canada.

item.title: Democrats Clash With Trump, as Political Tensions Rise on America’s 250th
item.description: Gavin Newsom and Wes Moore, potential 2028 contenders, cast President Trump’s record as a betrayal of American ideals, while the president disparaged their party as extreme.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Max Whittaker for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California in Sacramento in January. Mr. Newsom, a potential 2028 presidential candidate, has been a vocal critic of President Trump.

item.title: Huge Crowds Mass in Tehran for Ayatollah’s State Funeral
item.description: As days of public mourning ceremonies began, Iranians viewed the casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed at the start of the U.S.-Israeli attacks.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mourners gathered in Tehran to pay their respects to Ayatollah Ali Khamenei for his funeral on Saturday.

item.title: Prince Harry Will Travel to London, but Without His Family
item.description: The prince’s wife, Meghan, and their children could still join him for other parts of his trip outside the capital, a person close to the family said.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia and Caroline Houck
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:description: Prince Harry in Melbourne Park, Australia, in April.

item.title: Stanley M. Gartler, Pioneer in Cancer Research, Dies at 102
item.description: He provided the first convincing evidence that cancerous tumors develop from a single mutated cell and later helped expose contamination by cells taken from Henrietta Lacks.
item.dc:creator: Jeré Longman

item.title: Fireworks Canceled in Some Western Towns Because of Wildfire Danger
item.description: This Fourth of July, fires burning in several states have forced local officials to rethink celebration plans.
item.dc:creator: Madaleine Rubin

media:content.media:description: Silverton, Colo., one of many towns in the state forced to cancel their Fourth of July fireworks celebrations, in September.

item.title: Nathan’s Hot Dog Contest Captivates Coney Island Despite Heat Wave
item.description: Temperatures climbed above 90 degrees on Saturday, as Joey Chestnut and Miki Sudo once again reigned supreme.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy and Kaja Andric

media:content.media:description: Joey Chestnut was on pace to break his record early in the competition, but the heat appeared to slow him down. He still ate 66 hot dogs in 10 minutes.

item.title: Miles de mexicanos le piden al Niño Jesús una alegría en el Mundial
item.description: La tradición de vestir una estatuilla del Niño Jesús empezó en una iglesia de Ciudad de México hace casi 60 años. Se trasladó a la catedral para este Mundial y México aún no ha perdido.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Luis Antonio Rojas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Baby Jesus’s soccer uniform is size 0.

item.title: White Nationalists March in Washington Amid 250th Anniversary Celebrations
item.description: The group, wearing clothing associated with the organization Patriot Front, assembled near Capitol Hill before leaving the district.
item.dc:creator: Clarence Williams, Madaleine Rubin and Alan Feuer

media:content.media:description: A commuter sits as members of the group Patriot Front ride the metro on the Fourth of July in Washington, D.C.

item.title: Postales de la boda de Taylor Swift en Nueva York: fans, estrellas y más
item.description: La escena fuera de la boda de Taylor Swift en Manhattan tenía todo el dramatismo y la emoción de uno de sus álbumes.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher and Emma G. Fitzsimmons

media:content.media:credit: Lanna Apisukh for The New York Times

item.title: Funeral in Iran for Slain Ayatollah Begins Dayslong Public Mourning
item.description: Tens of thousands of people gathered for the state funeral of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed at the start of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran. The event in Tehran began several of public ceremonies mourning the country’s longtime supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: Axel Boada, Michael Anthony Adams, Abdi Latif Dahir, Mikhail Galustov and Emile Ducke
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce Unite in Marriage: An Imagined New York Times Wedding Announcement
item.description: This traditional wedding announcement marks the union of the pop star and football player, who exchanged vows on Friday at Madison Square Garden.
item.dc:creator: Rosalie R. Radomsky

media:content.media:description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce held a wedding ceremony at Madison Square Garden in Manhattan on July 3.

item.title: The Funeral of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei
item.description: Iran’s new leaders are commemorating Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, killed on the first day of the U.S.-Israeli war against the country, with a funeral unlike any other in recent history.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Emile Ducke for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mourners on Saturday at the Grand Mosalla mosque complex in Tehran, Iran’s capital, for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s funeral.

item.title: Atlantic Beach Pie Is the Ultimate Summertime Pie
item.description: The cookbook author and pie connoisseur Stacey Mei Yan Fong deems it so in the Bake Time Questionnaire.
item.dc:creator: Vaughn Vreeland

media:content.media:credit: Julia Gartland for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Bill Smith’s Atlantic Beach pie, adapted by Margaux Laskey.

item.title: The Unifying Speech We Won’t Get This Independence Day
item.description: America, we need a new beginning.
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat and Interesting Times

item.title: Fireworks and Trump Speech Cap Independence Day Celebrations
item.description: People across the United States marked the country’s 250th birthday even as extreme heat and storms disrupted some events. President Trump delivered a weather-delayed speech in the capital ahead of a huge fireworks show.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times

item.title: Huge Crowds Mass in Tehran for Ayatollah’s State Funeral
item.description: As days of public mourning ceremonies began, Iranians viewed the casket of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed four months ago at the start of the U.S.-Israeli attacks.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
